sessions.intent: Restructure LazyParamsGenerator into OBP shape — extract per-property emission into a polymorphic ActionProperty hierarchy without changing emitted output.

before.assumptions: Source generator stays (no reflection, no runtime compilation). Round 1 targets the 11-way per-property if/else only; markers, ExecuteAsync, helpers stay procedural. LangVersion=latest is already set, so records-with-inheritance work.
before.risk: Byte-for-byte output drift if branch priority or AppendLine ordering changes. Roslyn incremental caching could break if record equality is sloppy. Both mitigated by Phase 0 regression harness (pin .g.cs output before refactor, diff after).

actions.context: Design plan for round 1 of LazyParamsGenerator OBP refactor. Discriminated three alternatives (pre-generated files on disk, runtime Roslyn compilation, reflection-based ActionInvoker) and ruled them all out per Ingi's direction. Settled on extracting per-property emission only, with markers and ExecuteAsync kept procedural. Verified LangVersion=latest on PLang.Generators.csproj before committing to records-with-inheritance.

after.status: design complete, plan approved, awaiting coder handoff
after.health: no code touched, no risk introduced, regression contract (byte-for-byte golden diff) defined upfront
after.notes: Architect role ends here. Ingi approved committing golden snapshot to .bot/. Recommended next bot: coder directly (test-designer optional — golden diff is the test). changes.patch empty since architect-only session.

sessions.intent: Fresh-eyes review of v1; combine structural OBP refactor with contract simplification (helpers move to ActionBase, runtime kind-switch, thin generated ExecuteAsync) so the property hierarchy is built once in its final shape. Replaces v1 byte-for-byte golden harness with a runtime test matrix as the regression contract.

before.assumptions: Directory-as-API is a project-wide convention (Ingi confirmed) — internal generator structure follows it consistently. C# partial classes union base declarations, so generator can declare ActionBase as base while user partial declares interfaces. No existing action handler under App/modules/ inherits from a non-interface base (verified via grep).
before.risk: Bigger PR than v1; byte-for-byte safety is gone in favor of a runtime test matrix that must comprehensively cover ParamKind × {nullability, default, type-class}. Phase 0 matrix design is the load-bearing risk control.

actions.context: v2 supersedes v1. Fresh-eyes review surfaced three concerns: (1) thirteen @this folders looked cosmetic in a non-navigated project — resolved when Ingi confirmed directory-as-API is a project-wide commitment for LLM maintenance; (2) v1 was the second-most-impactful work because round 2's contract simplification would partially dismantle round 1's structure; (3) per-property logic splits across 5+ iteration sites in GenerateActionCode, not 3 as v1 implied. Concerns 2 and 3 argued for combining the rounds. v2 builds the property hierarchy in its final small shape (records carrying IsMatch + BuildResolveCall + optional emission slots), moves all helpers to App/modules/ActionBase/this.cs, introduces ParamKind enum to drive runtime dispatch, and shrinks per-class generated code from ~200 to ~50 lines. Regression contract: runtime test matrix (15–20 minimal action handlers covering every ParamKind × variant), not byte-for-byte golden files.

sessions.intent: Re-review v2 in conversation; supersede with v3 that drops ActionBase, drops ParamKind dispatch, moves resolution onto Data, puts lookup/scaffolding on Action.@this, collapses property hierarchy to two leaves, enforces Data<T>-only handler properties at build time.

before.assumptions: Action.Parameters is List<Data> (verified). Data<T> inherits Data; Data is essentially Data<object> for routing. Data.As<T>(Context) already exists as the typed-view primitive (line 389 of App/Data/this.cs). NeedsResolution flag exists on Data. Variables.ResolveDeep does the recursive walk today, with a discarded-return-value bug on the typed-list branch. llm.query handler uses Data<List<LlmMessage>> with %var% nested inside Content — verified the deep-resolution case is real.
before.risk: Bigger contract change than v2 (which still kept ParamKind). Behavior shift in Data.Value getter (now does work lazily) could affect debugger/audit paths that read .Value for inspection. Cycle risk: Data.Value → Variables.Get → returns Data → reads its Value (mitigated by NeedsResolution=false on already-resolved Variable Data). Migration: any handler with non-Data<T> property must convert (likely small scope based on read.cs and llm/query.cs samples).

actions.context: v3 supersedes v2 after substantive design conversation. Three architectural shifts: (1) ActionBase dropped — helpers belong on Action.@this (the data owner); handlers stay flat with no synthetic base class. (2) Resolution moves to Data.@this — Data.Value lazy getter does the variable lookup and recursive %var% substitution; Variables.ResolveDeep evaporates. (3) Property dispatch dissolves entirely — every Data<T> property emits one uniform line: __action.GetParameter(name, Context).As<T>(Context). Hierarchy collapses from 10 leaves to 2 (DataProperty, ProviderProperty). Build-time validation enforces Data<T>-only properties; runtime trusts the .pr. Regression contract stays: runtime test matrix (~22-25 handlers covering kind × type-shape × resolution combinations). Plan walks llm.query end-to-end as the grounding example — including deep-nested %var% inside List<LlmMessage>.
